task: Create a meme with a frog as the background and leave the only text with "Enjoy your life".
thoughts: I'll help you create a meme on Imgur with a frog background and "Enjoy your life" text. Let me start by navigating to Imgur.

I can see the Imgur homepage. I need to find the meme creation tool. Let me look for a way to create a meme. I'll check if there's a meme maker option. Let me look for a "New post" or meme creation button.

The meme generator is loaded. I can see a canvas with templates on the left. I need to find a frog background. Let me click on the canvas area to open the image upload menu, or first search for a frog image. Let me click on the upload area in the center.
I can see the Uploads panel with an "Upload" button and a link icon. Let me try using the link icon to add a frog image from a URL.
Let me try clicking on the Templates section to search for a frog template.
I see the Templates library. I need to find a frog background. Let me try clicking on the "Background" option in the left sidebar to see if there's a way to search for frog backgrounds.
I see the Background panel is showing a solid color picker. Let me check if there's an option to use an image as background by clicking the "Solid Color" dropdown.
The background only offers Solid Color or Gradient. I need to upload a frog image to the canvas instead. Let me close this dropdown and click on the upload area in the canvas.
Let me try using the link/URL option in the meme generator to add a frog image from a URL. I'll click on the link icon.
A URL input field appeared at the bottom. Let me type a frog image URL into it.
A URL input field appeared at the bottom. Let me type a frog image URL into it.

The frog image is now loaded as the background. I can see a beautiful yellow and black spotted frog. Now I need to change the "Bottom Text" to "Enjoy your life". Let me click on the "Bottom Text" to edit it.
The text element is selected. I can see the text formatting toolbar at the top. Let me double-click on the text to enter editing mode so I can change it.
Let me try clicking the "Edit" button in the toolbar to edit the text.

This opened the styling edit panel. I need to edit the actual text content. Let me look at the right panel - I see "Bottom" and "Text" tabs. Let me click on the "Text" tab on the right to find where I can edit the text content.
Let me click somewhere on the canvas to deselect, then try to directly double-click the "Bottom Text" to enter text editing mode.
I clicked on the image instead. Let me click outside the canvas to deselect everything, then double-click precisely on the "Bottom Text".
Let me scroll down a bit and double-click directly on the "Bottom Text" on the canvas.
The text element is selected but I'm not in edit mode. Let me try triple-clicking on the text to enter edit mode and select all text.
Let me try the three dots menu ("...") in the toolbar to see if there's an option to edit the text content.
I can see some icons appeared - let me delete this existing text first, then add new text with the correct content. Let me click the delete icon (trash can).
The "Bottom Text" has been deleted. Now I have a clean canvas with just the frog image. Let me click on "Text" in the left sidebar to add new text with "Enjoy your life".
I can see the Text panel with options to add text. Let me click on "Add a heading" to add the meme text.
A heading text has been added to the canvas showing "Add a heading". Now I need to double-click on it to enter edit mode and change the text to "Enjoy your life".
Let me try selecting all the text with Ctrl+A and then typing the new text.
Let me try selecting all the text with Ctrl+A and then typing the new text.
The Ctrl+A selected all elements instead. Let me click somewhere to deselect, then try a different approach to edit the text.
Good, everything is deselected. Now let me single-click on the "Add a heading" text on the canvas to select just the text element.
Good, the text element is selected. Now let me double-click directly on the text to enter text editing mode.
Let me try the paragraph/list icon in the toolbar which might give me a text editing option.
Let me close this and try pressing Enter to enter text editing mode on the selected text element.
Let me click once on the text to select it, then press Enter to enter editing mode.
Now let me press Enter to try to enter text editing mode.
Let me try right-clicking on the text to see if there's a context menu with edit options.
I see the context menu but no edit text option. Let me close this and try clicking the document-like icon next to "Edit" in the toolbar.
Let me close this menu and try a different approach. I'll click on the text element and just start typing to replace the text.
Let me click elsewhere to close the Paste menu.
Let me try double-clicking on the "Text" layer label in the right sidebar to enter text editing mode.
Let me try clicking on the "Aa" icon in the toolbar which might open text content editing.
Let me close this dropdown and try pressing F2 or typing directly after clicking on the text element.
